channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Drone footage above Gaza City shows the toll of 2 years of war
item.description: New drone footage shows what remains of Gaza City after two years of war as a ceasefire holds in its second day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins and US troops land in Israel as ceasefire holds
item.description: GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians returned to their Gaza neighborhoods Saturday, weaving through dust-shrouded streets as bulldozers clawed through the wreckage of two years of war and a ceasefire held in its second day. “Gaza is completely destroyed.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, SAM METZ, SARAH EL DEEB and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ivory Coast arrests 237 protesters amid rising tensions before presidential election
item.description: Authorities in the Ivory Coast have arrested at least 237 people during a protest against what activists call the country's authoritarian drift.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France's re-appointed prime minister Lecornu calls for calm amid political chaos
item.description: L'HAY-LES-ROSES, France (AP) — France’s newly re-appointed prime minister acknowledged Saturday that there weren’t ″a lot of candidates″ for his job — and that he might not last long in the post given the country’s deep political divides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greece's famed Parthenon free of scaffolding for first time in decades
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Walking along the broad pedestrian street that runs along the base of Athens’ famed Acropolis Hill, visitors can now enjoy something not seen in decades: an unobstructed, scaffolding-free view of the Parthenon temple. The scaffolding set up along the 5th century B.C.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power has been restored to over 800,000 residents in Kyiv, a day after Russia launched major attacks on Ukraine's power grid, causing widespread blackouts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Lee Keath, Julia Frankel And Jalal Bwaital, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chicago leaders, residents share mixed emotions and cautious hope after Israel-Hamas ceasefire announcement
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to a pause in their devastating two-year war and the release of the remaining hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize bets on Polymarket raise leak concerns, prompts probe
item.description: The last-minute bets, first reported by local outlets, are a cause for concern for the Norwegian officials in charge of awarding the honor
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, The Hill

item.title: Republican leaders backed Venezuela's Machado for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Sen. Rick Scott, R-Fla., signed a letter supporting opposition leader María Corina Machado.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Nobel winner dedicates peace prize to people of Venezuela, Trump
item.description: This year's Nobel Peace Prize winner, Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado, has dedicated her award to the people of her country and to President Trump for his "decisive support” for democracy in Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Surina Venkat, The Hill

item.title: Melania Trump in talks with Putin on reuniting Ukrainian children with families
item.description: First lady Melania Trump said Friday she has been in communication with Russian President Vladimir Putin about reuniting Ukrainian children displaced by war with their families.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens countermeasures after China tightens grip on rare earths, says 'no reason' to meet Xi
item.description: President Trump on Friday threatened an increase in tariffs on China and other potential countermeasures after Beijing tightened control over rare earth elements and critical mineral exports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO begins a big nuclear exercise next week. Protecting the weapons is a key part of the drill
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO will hold its major annual nuclear exercise next week, the alliance’s chief announced Friday, with an important part of the drill to focus on protecting the weapons before they're ever used.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taiwan's president pledges to build air defense system in face of China threat
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan will accelerate the building of a “Taiwan Shield” or “T-Dome” air defense system in the face of the military threat from China, its leader said Friday. President Lai Ching-te also pledged to raise defense spending to more than 3% of GDP and to reach 5% by 2030.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: King Mohammed VI set to speak as Gen Z protesters demand reforms in Morocco
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco's elusive King Mohammed VI inaugurates parliament Friday in a speech with far higher stakes than in recent years, as anti-government protests sweep the nation's cities, questioning the government's spending priorities.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Israel and Hamas agree to a ceasefire, release of hostages and prisoners
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to a pause in their devastating two-year war and the release of the remaining hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump pushing for drastic reduction in refugees allowed into US
item.description: "Our concern is that this could turn what has long been a globally responsive humanitarian system into one that overwhelmingly favors a single group.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: US diplomat fired over relationship with woman accused of Chinese Communist Party ties
item.description: The dismissal is believed to be the first of its kind for violating a ban on such relationships that was introduced late last year under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Celebrations in Israel, Gaza at first peace deal announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump says the remaining Israeli hostages in Gaza may be released as soon as Monday.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Israel seizes another Gaza flotilla
item.description: Israel intercepted another flotilla attempting to break the naval blockade and reach Gaza. This comes just days after another fleet was intercepted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza peace talks appear to gain momentum on the war's anniversary
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Peace talks between Israel and Hamas resumed at an Egyptian resort city on Tuesday, the two-year anniversary of the militant group's surprise attack on Israel that started the war that has killed tens of thousands of Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu, on 2-year mark of Oct. 7 Hamas terror attack, says Israel 'not broken,' vows to bring hostages home
item.description: Prime Minister Netanyahu marked two years since Hamas’ Oct. 7 assault in a statement issued on X, honoring the victims and vowing Israel’s war for survival continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel and Hamas launch indirect talks in Egypt on eve of Gaza war anniversary
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israeli and Hamas officials launched indirect talks Monday at an Egyptian resort on a U.S.-drafted peace plan to end the ruinous war in Gaza on the eve of its second anniversary. The talks, which went on for several hours, unfolded amid many questions about the plan presented by U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rare ancient artwork vanishes from tomb in Egypt: Report
item.description: The artwork, a 4,000-year-old limestone relief, was reported to have disappeared over the weekend from a Fifth Dynasty tomb.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Greta Thunberg is among flotilla activists deported from Israel. Others remain in prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli authorities said Monday they deported to Greece and Slovakia another 171 people detained for taking part in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla, including prominent Swedish activist Greta Thunberg.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says it struck Russian ammunition plant, oil terminal and weapons depot
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Long-range Ukrainian drones and missiles hit a major Russian ammunition plant, a key oil terminal and an important weapons depot behind the front line, Ukraine's president and military said Monday, as Kyiv cranked up pressure on Moscow’s military logistics.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rescuers in India search for missing after landslides kill 24 in Darjeeling region
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Rescuers were scouring India's northeastern tea-growing hill district of Darjeeling on Monday for those reported missing a day after landslides triggered by heavy downpours killed at least 24 people, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
